challenges.title: Basic Class with Encapsulation
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that defines a class called `Rectangle` with private data members `width` and `height` (both double). Include a public constructor that takes width and height parameters, a getter method `getArea()` that returns width * height, and a getter `getPerimeter()` that returns 2 * (width + height). In `main`, create a `Rectangle` object and print its area and perimeter. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'class' in cpp and 'Rectangle' in cpp, 'Define a class called Rectangle'
assert 'private' in cpp, 'Use private access specifier to encapsulate data members'
assert 'public' in cpp, 'Use public access specifier for the constructor and methods'
assert 'getArea' in cpp, 'Define a getArea() method'
assert 'getPerimeter' in cpp, 'Define a getPerimeter() method'
assert 'width' in cpp and 'height' in cpp, 'The class should have width and height members'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Print the area and perimeter in main'
hints: Use private: for data members and public: for the constructor and methods
Constructor syntax: Rectangle(double w, double h) { width = w; height = h; }

challenges.title: Class with Constructor, Destructor, and Methods
challenges.description: Write a C++ program that defines a class called `Logger` with a private string member `name` and a private int member `messageCount`. Include a constructor that takes a name parameter and initializes `messageCount` to 0, a destructor that prints a shutdown message, a `log` method that increments `messageCount` and prints a message, and a `getCount` method. In `main`, create a `Logger`, call `log` at least twice, and print the message count. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'class' in cpp and 'Logger' in cpp, 'Define a class called Logger'
assert 'private' in cpp, 'Use private access specifier for data members'
assert '~Logger' in cpp, 'Define a destructor with ~Logger'
assert 'log' in cpp.split('class')[1] if 'class' in cpp else False, 'Define a log method in the class'
assert 'getCount' in cpp, 'Define a getCount method to return the message count'
assert 'messageCount' in cpp or 'message_count' in cpp, 'Track the number of messages with a counter'
assert cpp.count('.log(') >= 2 or cpp.count('->log(') >= 2, 'Call the log method at least twice in main'
hints: Destructor syntax: ~Logger() { cout << name << " shutting down" << endl; }
Increment messageCount inside the log method each time it is called
